{{Quote|A small gift from Count Dooku. A delicacy. Take heart: the Yanthans who remove the venom sacs rarely make a mistake. And even if they do—it is said to be a good death.|Asajj Ventress|<ref name="Cestus">''[[The Cestus Deception]]''</ref>}}
The '''Yanthans''' were beings<ref name="Cestus" /> [[Homeworld/Legends|from]] the [[Yantha system/Legends|Yantha system]]<ref name="Planetnamia" /> who expertly excised venom sacs from [[windsnake]]s, [[Snake/Legends|creatures]] native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ploo II/Legends|Ploo II]].<ref name="Cestus" /> Around [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="TERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[The Cestus Deception]]'' to around [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].</ref> [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]]<ref name="Cestus" /> [[Commander/Legends|Commander]]<ref name="Jedi Trial">''[[Jedi Trial]]''</ref> [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] presented the [[X'Ting]] criminal [[Trillot]] with two windsnakes that had had their venom sacs removed by Yanthans, noting that they were a gift from her superior, [[Count of Serenno/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]]. Ventress indicated that Yanthans rarely made mistakes with the removal of the sacs but described the instances in which they were unsuccessful to be a "good [[Death/Legends|death]]."<ref name="Cestus" />

==Behind the scenes==
Yanthans were mentioned in the [[2004]] novel ''[[The Cestus Deception]]'', written by [[Steven Barnes]].<ref name="Cestus" />

*''[[The Cestus Deception]]'' {{1stm}}
